mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and earnings revisions that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in residential housing starts and revolving consumer credit indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of forward 60-day windows, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects recovering capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% annualized confirms an economic slowdown that compresses corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in the majority of quarters where GDP growth falls below the 1.5% threshold, or the signal exhibits no incremental predictive power over a simple momentum baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains consumer spending power and corporate revenue trajectories, generating consistent positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day investment horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return distribution shows no statistically significant positive skew when PAYEMS growth exceeds 100,000 jobs per month, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a tightening federal funds rate compress real yields and trigger valuation multiple contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both CPI and the federal funds rate rise simultaneously, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for VIX futures levels.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls coupled with a rising unemployment rate signal an active labor market deterioration, forcing institutional risk reduction and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where the dual labor market deterioration condition is met, or the joint signal fails to improve Sharpe ratios over a 2-year walk-forward test.
